articles.title: 80% OF IPOS ARE BROKEN
articles.summary: The Wall St Journal recently looked into IPOs. 80% of IPOs since 2020 are trading below their IPO price. A bad IPO market creates stress across the entire venture stack. If people aren’t making money at the top, things like price will need to change further down at the bottom.

articles.title: THE SOFTWARE INDUSTRY'S PRODUCTIVITY BOOM: ANALYZING REVENUE PER EMPLOYEE TRENDS
articles.summary: Modern businesses produce 2x more revenue per employee than their decade old selves. This article dives into the stats behind 13 public software companies that have meaningfully increased their RPE.

articles.title: UNPACKING OPENAI’S DEV DAY
articles.summary: A breakdown of the major announcements from OpenAI’s Dev Day and its implications on the technology industry.

articles.title: FOUR SALES COMPENSATION TACTICS FOR CONSUMPTION-BASED GTM
articles.summary: Made popular by the likes of Google Azure, AWS, Twilio, and Stripe, consumption based pricing is gaining popularity. Particularly in a market where buyers don’t want to pay for more than they’re using. For your sales org, it means the lines between pre-sales and post-sales are blurred, and you’ll need effective internal tooling to measure incremental ARR from accounts.

articles.title: WHY YOU SHOULD CARE ABOUT YOUR EMPLOYEES’ LINKEDIN CONNECTIONS
articles.summary: This study found that companies whose real-world employee connections put them at the center of their professional communities performed better than peer companies whose workforces were less well-connected. Perhaps something to start to look into during your hiring process, even for non-customer-facing roles.

articles.title: THE CFO TECH STACK GOING INTO 2024
articles.summary: This blog explores the finance stacks of a handful of top startups like Brex, Webflow, and Public. The finance stack has gotten some love over recent years, with more than 150 venture-backed options for FP&A alone. Pass this one on to your finance team.

articles.title: GPT-4 TURBO NOTE TAKER (PRODUCT)
articles.summary: Let AI automate your meeting notes using GPT-4 Turbo.

articles.title: LEBESGUE (PRODUCT)
articles.summary: Scrape global marketing data to make your marketing work.

articles.title: BYEDISPUTE (PRODUCT)
articles.summary: Prevent disputes before getting banned from Stripe.

articles.title: THE RISE OF ADVOCATES IN SELLING SOFTWARE
articles.summary: The importance of developer relations for technical products is increasing. As competition gets hotter, developers need social proof from their peers before they’ll use a new product. Engineering-led sales can be a way to extend the successful pattern of founder-led sales before you hire your sales team.

articles.title: TO GO 0 TO 1, FIRST GO -1 TO 0
articles.summary: In the early stages of South Park Commons, there was a lack of clear language to describe its purpose. This phase, now termed "-1 to 0," represents a crucial period where ambitious technologists and founders determine their future focus, a process more challenging and crucial than often recognized, requiring a shift from mere talent to developing deep conviction about what and how to build.

articles.title: AGI’S IMPACT ON TECH, SAAS VALUATIONS
articles.summary: The rapid advancement of AI, particularly AGI, could significantly impact the valuation of SaaS and tech companies by increasing competition, compressing margins, and reducing the demand for traditional project management and CRUD apps, while also challenging incumbents with new AI-native startups and self-serve app creation, ultimately leading to a shift in the software value chain.

articles.title: COPILOT IS AN INCUMBENT BUSINESS MODEL
articles.summary: The Copilot AI business model enhances existing workflows for efficiency without creating new markets or disrupting lower ends, but its true disruptive potential lies in reimagining workflows, a challenge that could unlock significantly larger market opportunities.

articles.title: AMAZON STRIKES DEAL WITH SNAP TO LET USERS BUY ITS PRODUCTS FROM ADS ON SNAPCHAT
articles.summary: Amazon has partnered with Snapchat to enable in-app shopping, allowing users to purchase products directly from ads within the social app using linked Amazon accounts.

articles.title: BEYOND THE SURFACE AREA OF LUCK
articles.summary: The "surface area of luck" theory posits that actively engaging in diverse and valuable activities, expanding one's network, and consistently applying effort over time can increase the likelihood of encountering serendipitous opportunities.
